§ 01Engagement

Dear {{appraiserName}},

Forhemit Transition Stewardship is facilitating a 100% ESOP transaction for {{companyName}}. The independent ESOP trustee ({{trusteeName}}, {{trusteeFirm}}) has selected your firm to perform the Fair Market Value appraisal. This briefing provides the deal context, scope, and requested timeline.

Client relationship: The appraiser is engaged by and reports to the independent ESOP trustee — not to Forhemit, not to the company, and not to the seller. Forhemit coordinates the process and provides the data package, but does not influence the valuation methodology or conclusions.
§ 02Company Profile
{{companyName}}
{{industry}}
{{location}}
{{entityType}}
{{foundedYear}}
{{employeeCount}}
{{revenueTTM}}
{{ebitdaReported}}
{{revenueTrend}}
{{customerConcentration}}
§ 03Appraisal Scope

The trustee requires the following from the appraisal engagement:

A.Quality of Earnings (QofE). Normalized EBITDA analysis with detailed adjustments for owner compensation, personal expenses, related-party transactions, and non-recurring items.
B.Valuation report. Fair Market Value determination using income approach (DCF) and market approach (guideline public company and guideline transaction methods). Report must comply with ASA and IRS standards.
C.FMV opinion. Concluded Fair Market Value of 100% of the equity on a controlling, marketable basis. This is the price the ESOP will pay.
D.Adequacy of consideration. Opinion on whether the purchase price is fair to the ESOP and its participants from a financial standpoint.
E.Solvency opinion. Confirmation that the company will be solvent after the transaction (able to meet debts as they come due).
§ 04Data Package Provided

Forhemit will provide the following data package to support the appraisal:

013 years of federal tax returns with all schedules
023 years of financial statements (P&L and balance sheet)
03Year-to-date financials through {{ytdDate}}
04Revenue breakdown by customer and product/service line
05Expense detail with owner compensation breakdown
06Employee census with compensation and benefits
07Existing debt schedule with terms and collateral
08Organizational chart and key person assessment
09Industry overview and competitive position summary
103-year financial projections (if available)
CPA coordination: The company's CPA ({{cpaName}}, {{cpaFirm}}) prepared the financial statements and tax returns. Forhemit will facilitate introductions if the appraiser needs to discuss accounting methods, adjustments, or working papers directly with the CPA.
§ 05Timeline
{{dataPackageDate}}
{{qofeDraftDate}}
{{valuationDraftDate}}
10 business days
{{finalReportDate}}
{{gate2Date}} — Valuation confirmed
Note: The 120-day transaction timeline depends on the appraisal being completed on schedule. Delays in the QofE or valuation report directly impact the closing date. Please flag any timeline risks early.
